mnemonics.title: FRIEND Properties
mnemonics.content:
F - Fulfills specific needs
R - Restricted access only
I - Inheritance doesn't apply
E - Entire class access
N - Not transitive
D - Declared anywhere section

Use: Remember FRIEND as acronym for key properties of friend classes.

mnemonics.title: Friend Class Access
mnemonics.content:
FRAP - Friend Reaches All Private

Use: Remember FRAP to recall that friend classes can access all private members.

mnemonics.title: Three NOs of Friendship
mnemonics.content:
N - Not inherited
N - Not transitive
N - Not reciprocal (one-way)

Use: Remember the Three NOs when working with friend classes.

acronyms.expansion: F - Fulfills need | R - Restricted | I - Independent of section | E - Entire access | N - Not inherited | D - Direct private access
acronyms.usage: Remembering properties of friend classes in C++

acronyms.expansion: Not Overused | Not Inherited | Not Transitive

visualTips.visualization: Draw an arrow from Class A to Class B labeled 'friend'. Show arrow only goes one direction, not back. This represents A grants B access, but B doesn't grant A access.
Draw three circles: A, B, C. Draw arrow A→B (friend) and B→C (friend). Show a big X between A and C with label 'NOT friend'. This shows friendship doesn't chain.
Draw a parent class (Friend) with arrow to Child class. Put a shield with X on the arrow from Parent to Child. Show that even though parent is friend, child is NOT automatically a friend.
Draw a class box with three sections: public, protected, private. Show friend class passing through the wall (boundary) to access private section directly.

analogies.analogy: Think of a friend class like giving a spare key to your house. The key holder (friend class) can enter private spaces (house), but doesn't mean everyone else can, and giving key to one person doesn't give their family (derived) automatic access.

analogies.concept: Friendship is like a VIP pass
analogies.analogy: A friend class is like a VIP pass at a concert. It grants special access to restricted areas (private members), but this VIP status isn't passed to others (not inherited) and doesn't make you friends with other VIPs (not transitive).

Think of a friend class as a bouncer who can enter the staff area (private). Other regular visitors (non-friend classes) cannot enter. The bouncer's badge (friendship) isn't given to their team members automatically.
